fix: LLM 모델 72B→32B 변경 (A40 단일 카드 OOM 해결)

- Qwen2.5-72B: 4-bit 시 ~44GB → A40 44.4GB 초과
- Qwen2.5-32B: 4-bit 시 ~18GB → A40에 여유 있게 로드 가능
- VRAM 여유분으로 KV 캐시 및 임베딩 처리 가능

- chain.py: device_map='auto' → device_map={'': 0} 로 변경
  (4-bit 양자화는 CPU 오프로딩 미지원, GPU 강제 고정)

commit.message:
Initial commit: EZConfig - deployment vehicles for vibe coders

125 vehicles across 12 categories, organized by outcome not technology.
Static site (vanilla HTML/CSS/JS) with quiz flow, browsable catalog,
searchable/filterable vehicle cards, detail modals with copy-paste
prompt templates, and personal narrative section.

15 prompt templates for the most common vehicles.
GitHub Actions workflow for Pages deployment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: muhammedgaygisiz <54054345+muhammedgaygisiz@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Verify snapshot naming for xunit v3 compatibility (#10052)

## Summary

`Verify.XunitV3` auto-detects `[Theory]` parameters from the xunit v3
test context and appends them to snapshot file paths. This breaks
snapshot matching because TemplateVerifier already manages naming
uniqueness via `ScenarioName`. It can also cause path-too-long errors,
particularly in Helix where the directory structure is deeper.

This is a behavioral difference between `Verify.Xunit` (xunit v2) and
`Verify.XunitV3` (xunit v3) — the v2 package does not auto-append
parameters, while the v3 package does regardless of test runner mode.

## Fix

Switch from `UseTypeName` + `UseMethodName` to `UseFileName`, which
replaces the entire type+method+parameters naming in a single call. This
prevents Verify from appending auto-detected parameters.

`UseFileName` is the only Verify API that fully controls the output path
— there is no more targeted API to disable parameter auto-appending.
Snapshot file names remain functionally identical; the parameters
previously managed via `UseMethodName` are now pre-baked into the file
name string.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Sistema de agentes de tráfego pago para Corte Imóveis

Sistema multi-agente para gestão de tráfego pago de imobiliária
no litoral gaúcho (Capão da Canoa e Xangri-Lá).

Stack:
- CrewAI para orquestração de agentes
- Gemini 2.0 Flash como LLM
- Supabase (PostgreSQL + pgvector)
- Chainlit para interface de chat
- Meta Ad Library + Marketing API

Agentes:
- Pesquisador: Inteligência competitiva via Ad Library
- Copywriter: Geração de copies A/B/C
- Gestor: Criação e otimização de campanhas

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement all 16 changes: voting fixes, view rewrites, tests, ErrorBoundary

- CHANGE 1: Remove local seededRandom from 4 feature views, import from @/lib/utils
- CHANGE 2: Fix voting.ts Array<number> generics and applyCliqueWeighting no-op
- CHANGE 3: Replace Math.random IDs in votingAudit.ts with uuid v4
- CHANGE 4: Add useRef seeding guard, useCallback handlers, transparencyLog KV, createTransparencyLogEntry on submit
- CHANGE 5: Rewrite ChartsView with real fanVotes data and seededRandom from utils
- CHANGE 6: Rewrite FanVoteView with genre filter tabs, tier badges, Reset All button
- CHANGE 7: Rewrite CategoriesView with fanVotes prop, real credits, ScrollArea for all tracks
- CHANGE 8: Rewrite ARDashboardView with Recharts BarChart tier distribution, real computed data
- CHANGE 9: Rewrite AIScoutView using generateAIPrediction from voting.ts
- CHANGE 10: Update TransparencyLog with search filter, show all entries (not just user's)

Integrate StarRocks lakehouse with Iceberg/AWS Glue catalog

- Set create_irsa_service_account=True in OLEKSAuthBinding so the
  'starrocks' ServiceAccount is pre-created with the
  eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn annotation before the StarRocks operator
  starts. This enables IRSA credential injection into FE/CN/BE pods via
  the EKS pod-identity webhook.

- When starrocks:enable_data_lake_integration is true, attach the
  data_lake_query_engine_iam_policy from the data_warehouse stack to the
  IRSA role, granting Glue Data Catalog and data-lake S3 access.

- Export iceberg_catalog_sql as a stack output containing the
  CREATE EXTERNAL CATALOG SQL to run against the StarRocks FE (port 9030)
  after deployment, using instance-profile auth backed by IRSA.

- Add depends_on=[starrocks_auth_binding] to the Helm release so the
  annotated SA exists before the operator first reconciles.

- Add QA stack config: enable_data_lake_integration=true (references
  infrastructure.aws.data_warehouse.QA).

- Add CI stack YAML with smaller resources and
  enable_data_lake_integration=false (no data_warehouse.CI stack yet;
  flip to true once that stack is provisioned).

- Add Production stack YAML with production-grade resources and
  enable_data_lake_integration=true (references
  infrastructure.aws.data_warehouse.Production).

commit.message:
feat(qdrant-cloud): add per-node RAM target config; set production to 16Gi

Add `desired_ram_gib` stack config support alongside the existing
`desired_disk_gib`, using the same additional-resource-slab pattern.
When set, the delta above the base package RAM is requested via a
`resource_type=ram` entry in `resource_configurations`.

Also refactored the resource-configuration builder into a single unified
block that accumulates both disk and RAM slabs before assigning to
`node_resource_configurations`.

Set `qdrant-cloud:desired_ram_gib: "16"` on the Production stack
(mx2 base is 8Gi, so this requests 8Gi of additional RAM per node).

commit.message:
xpro celery fix  (#4359)

* fix: allowed for an unspecified queue name in celery worker configurations.

* fix: separate queue_name (CLI -Q filter) from worker_name (K8s/KEDA naming)

Address PR review feedback:
- Replace the @property worker_name (which had a hardcoded 'celery' fallback)
  with an explicit worker_name field that derives from queue_name when not set
- Add field_validator rejecting empty/whitespace strings on queue_name and
  worker_name, preventing silent misconfiguration
- Raise ValueError when both queue_name and worker_name are None so the caller
  must be explicit rather than relying on an opaque default
- Update xpro to set worker_name='default' (preserves KEDA scaling on the
  'default' Redis list) without setting queue_name (no -Q filter passed to
  the celery worker command)

commit.message:
fix: replace WPF JumpList with Shell COM API (ICustomDestinationList)

The WPF System.Windows.Shell.JumpList wrapper is unreliable on .NET 10:
it silently discards every custom-category item when the icon path is
missing or when its internal AUMID matching fails, leaving the taskbar
right-click menu with no 'Connections' category.

Changes:
- Add NativeJumpList.cs: builds the jump list directly via the Windows
  Shell COM API (ICustomDestinationList / IShellLinkW / IPropertyStore).
  Each connection becomes an IShellLink with Title set via IPropertyStore
  (PKEY_Title), making the name visible in the jump list.  AppendCategory
  commits a 'Connections' custom category; AbortList is called on any
  error so Windows shell state is never left inconsistent.
- App.xaml.cs: remove WPF JumpList code and System.Windows.Shell import;
  call NativeJumpList.Rebuild() instead.  Guard the default icon path
  with File.Exists so a missing .ico never causes the entire list to be
  rejected silently.  Replace the swallowed catch{} with LogJumpListError()
  which appends the exception type and message to
  %APPDATA%\PowerTerminal\logs\startup.log alongside the existing
  ConfigService startup diagnostic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(connection-manager): resize off, auto-select first, icons in list, dark combobox

- ResizeMode=NoResize on ConnectionManagerWindow
- Auto-select first connection on open (sets Selected + loads Editing form)
- Add icon (image or fallback glyph) before each connection in the ListBox
- ComboBox IsEnabled=False trigger now explicitly keeps SurfaceBrush background
  (WPF can fall back to white when a parent Grid is disabled)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(k8s): expand duplicate metrics port validation to cover all three conflict paths

The validate_no_duplicate_metrics_port validator on OLApplicationK8sConfig
only checked that extra_container_ports did not contain an entry named
'metrics'. This left three paths through which a duplicate containerPort
could reach the pod spec undetected:

1. extra_container_ports with a different name but the same port number as
   metrics_port — two identical containerPort values on the main container.
2. extra_sidecar_containers containing a container that declares a port
   named 'metrics' or with container_port == metrics_port — duplicate port
   name or number across containers in the same pod (the pattern that would
   fire if the edxapp vector sidecar were ever given an explicit metrics
   port).
3. application_port == metrics_port — two containerPort entries with the
   same number on the main application container.

All three gaps are now caught at config-validation time (pulumi preview)
with a clear error message pointing to the fix.

Adds 12 new unit tests covering every branch, including a
test_sidecar_without_ports_ok test that confirms the current edxapp vector
sidecar (no ports declared) continues to pass validation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: ol-infrastructure

commit.message:
fix: remove explicit AUMID that broke taskbar jump list

Setting a custom AppUserModelID mismatches with the AUMID Windows
assigns to the pinned shortcut (which is derived from the exe path).
That mismatch causes Windows to silently discard the Connections
jump list category. Removing the explicit AUMID lets both the app
and its pinned shortcut share the same auto-derived AUMID.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: regenerate Chart.lock, clean up .gitignore, add Chart.lock CI validation (#268)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix three real bugs from code review

1. Reserved-name copyback leak (config._exec_py_async)
   The final self._py_namespace.update() used a fixed-name exclusion
   ('_tgdb_RSVD_run_script') instead of a prefix check, so any other
   _tgdb_RSVD_* name produced by the wrapper could leak into the
   persistent user namespace.
   Fix: change exclusion to startswith(_TGDB_RESERVED_PREFIX).
   Also introduce a module-level _TGDB_RESERVED_PREFIX constant so the
   prefix string is defined in one place and referenced everywhere
   (wrapper code, exec lookup, and copyback).

2. PTY file-descriptor leak in gdb_controller.start()
   mi_master_fd and mi_slave_fd were opened before PtyProcess.spawn().
   On spawn failure both fds were leaked.
   Fix: wrap spawn in try/except; close both fds on failure before
   re-raising. Also move the fd assignments to after a successful spawn
   so the terminate() path never sees half-initialised fds.

3. Unguarded bare lambda in asyncio.get_running_loop().call_later()
   The 0.1-second delayed location request used a raw lambda calling
   gdb.request_current_location(); any exception raised there would be
   swallowed silently by the event loop with no context.
   Fix: introduce _safe_request_location() which wraps the call in
   try/except, and use that in the call_later lambda.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use native PandaSet ring-beam supervision

Switch PandaSet LiDAR supervision to a reconstructed native ring_id x
beam_id grid and align LiDAR rendering with per-ring azimuth offsets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: anthropic-code-agent[bot] <242468646+Claude@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Nic <qianyong@api7.ai>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Load rc file async post-mount; drop cgdb paths; support --rcfile NONE

The rc file was previously loaded synchronously in TGDBApp.__init__,
before the app was mounted and the event loop was running.  This meant:
- await expressions in :python blocks silently failed
- tgdb.screen.split() and similar async API calls did nothing
- The user saw the wrong initial layout

Now rc loading is an async task launched at the end of on_mount, so
every rc command runs exactly as if the user typed it interactively
after startup — including await, tgdb.screen.*, asyncio.sleep(), etc.

Other changes:
- Remove /cgdbrc and ~/.cgdb/cgdbrc from the candidate list;
  the only supported default path is ~/.config/tgdb/tgdbrc (XDG).
- Support --rcfile NONE (all-caps) to skip rc loading entirely.
- Update --rcfile help text and module docstrings to remove cgdb refs.
- Add ConfigParser.default_rc_path() helper used by _load_rc_async.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
